task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application window or fullscreen interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the chess activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific chess game interface within GCompris is open, identifiable by the GCompris UI elements (like the bottom control bar with home/exit icons) surrounding a chessboard.
White pieces (typically at the bottom of the board) have been moved from their initial starting positions on ranks 1 and 2.
Black pieces have been moved from their initial starting positions on ranks 7 and 8, indicating the computer has played its turns.

task_completion.description: Complete at least 4 full moves (4 white, 4 black)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The board state shows a level of development consistent with at least 4 complete turns. This means a combination of at least 4 moves by white and 4 by black (e.g., multiple pawns advanced, knights/bishops developed, or pieces captured).

task_completion.description: Capture and save a screenshot of the board state to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a screenshot command (e.g., 'scrot /home/ga/chess_progress.png', 'import', etc.) or a GUI screenshot tool saving the file exactly to '/home/ga/chess_progress.png'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the actual GCompris software
integrity.visual_evidence: The chess game must be inside the GCompris interface (look for the characteristic cartoonish UI, bottom navigation bar with a home/exit button). It should not be a web browser playing chess (like chess.com) or a different desktop chess application.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not download or spoof the screenshot
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show the agent interacting with the GUI to play the game. There should be no terminal commands like 'wget' or 'curl' downloading a pre-made chess image to the target path.

integrity.description: Verify the board state reflects a genuine game
integrity.visual_evidence: The arrangement of pieces on the board should reflect a sequence of legal chess moves, not randomly scattered pieces or an impossible board state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected screenshot path is exactly '/home/ga/chess_progress.png'. The task requires at least 4 complete moves, which means 4 moves by white and 4 responses by the computer (black). A developed board state should show approximately 8 total half-moves, meaning multiple pawns or minor pieces (knights/bishops) have moved from their starting ranks. The chess activity is part of GCompris, so the GCompris UI must be visible.
